Items.Title: 对 CSS HTML 和 JavaScript 感到困惑？ 他们如何将一切联系在一起 | Puzzled About CSS HTML and JavaScript? How They Can Tie Everything Together
Items.Description:
同时学习 JavaScript、HTML 和 CSS？ 函数、循环、变量、数组、对象、字符串……不胜枚举！ 作为初学者，听起来相当压倒性对吧？！ 尤其是当大部分都像一门外语时！ 你猜怎么着，它们是语言！ 您正在学习如何为应用程序编写规则以执行您的要求。 如果不了解基本知识，你怎么能做到这一点？ 还有，你怎么能让它看起来像样的？ 耐心的蚂蚱！

虽然有时它可能会让人不知所措，但希望它能让你感到安慰，因为它们可以通过某种凝聚力聚集在一起（一旦你对它们如何单独工作有了更基本的了解）。 让我试着给你一个非常简短的高级理解，每个人分别做什么，然后让我在最后把它们联系在一起。 完全免责声明：考虑到这个技术意见来自一个新手软件工程专业的学生。

这是网站最基本、最原始的大纲。 它提供了一个非常粗略的占位符布局以显示给用户。 想想文本、段落、分隔线、按钮、列表、图像。 您创建和显示所有这些项目（和其他项目）并在 HTML 中定义它们的一些样式，但这个过程非常繁琐。

如今，如果没有其他语言的帮助来告诉他们如何看（想想 CSS），现在只使用 HTML 并不容易。 计算、数学、任务？ 也不是那么多，想想那些项目的 JavaScript。 那么有什么好的思考方式呢？ 也许将 HTML 更多地视为客户端界面所基于的骨架或结构。 虽然 HTML 不做某些事情（很好或根本不做），但它可以指向可以做的事情：JavaScript 和 CSS。

 <html> <head> <meta charset="utf-8" /> <title>My Title</title> <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="styles.css" /> <script type="text/javascript" src="./src/index.js"></script> </head> <body> </body> </html>

需要对变量进行一些数学运算吗？ 或者想要监控鼠标点击然后执行任务？ 循环并从包含数千条信息的对象中提取数据？ 它可以只用几行代码循环。 此 JavaScript 代码使用 forEach 方法循环遍历所有 menuData 项并自动创建 HTML 数据，这些数据将在调用 buildMenu 函数时显示在网站上。 这可能是数百行代码，需要在 JavaScript 中手动输入半打代码。

 function buildMenu(menuData) { let menuList = document.querySelector("#menu-items"); menuData.forEach(item => { let menuListItem = document.createElement("span"); menuListItem.textContent = item.name; menuList.appendChild(menuListItem); }); }

如果您的界面中有评论部分并希望用户能够将这些评论直接添加到界面中，那会怎样？ 在您在文本输入表单中输入一些文本后，JavaScript 可能会监视“发表评论”按钮，以便您单击它。 它可以将此数据存储在一个变量（或其他地方）中，并告诉 HTML 使用您刚刚在文本输入表单中输入的文本创建一个新段落。

不透明度（透明度）、网格对齐、将字体系列分配给页面的特定区域。 想要屏幕左半边的文本框有圆边边框吗？ 让 CSS 轻松地告诉网络浏览器如何显示它。

虽然这些属性需要手动输入到 HTML 中的每个项目中，但它可以在 CSS 中简化为几行。 一种方法是使用 css 选择器从本质上过滤该类型的所有项目并分配首选的视觉特征。 这里的 CSS 代码选择所有 p（段落）项目并在六行中为它们设置一些属性。

 p { color: red; background-color: black; font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-weight: bold; border: #fff solid; border-radius: 5px; }

想要在调整窗口大小时适当且动态地缩放项目？ 你猜对了，CSS 已经涵盖了你。 我会为你省去如何做到这一点的细节，只要知道 CSS 使它成为可能。

概括
希望您能够对这 3 个中的每一个单独执行的操作有所了解，并看到它们可以很好地补充其他工具，以创建美观且功能强大的工具。

希望外卖是：

所有 3 : 结合在一起时更加强大和高效



原文:

Learning JavaScript, HTML and CSS at the same time? Functions, loops, variables, arrays, objects, strings… the list goes on! Sounds pretty overwhelming as a beginner right?! Especially when most of it is like a foreign language! Guess what, they are languages! You are learning how to write the rules for the application to do what you are asking it to. How can you do this without understanding the basic groundwork? Also how can you make it look decent? Patience grasshopper!

While it may be overwhelming at times, hopefully it will bring you some comfort to know that they can all come together with some cohesion (once you get a more basic understanding of how they work individually). Let me try to give you a very brief high level understanding of what each does individually and then allow me to tie it together at the end. Full disclaimer: consider that this technical opinion is coming from a novice software engineering student.

HTML
This is the most basic and primitive outline of website. It gives a very rough layout of placeholders to display to the user. Think text, paragraphs, dividers, buttons, lists, images. You create and display all these items (and others) and define some of their styling in HTML but the process is very cumbersome.

Using HTML only is not easy on the eye these days without the help of other languages to tell them how to look (think CSS). Calculations, math, tasks? Also not so much, think of JavaScript for those items. So what is a good way to think about it? Maybe think of HTML as more of the skeleton or structure that a client interface is based on. While HTML does not do some things (well or at all) it can point to the items that can: JavaScript and CSS.

<html>
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8" />
  <title>My Title</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="styles.css" />
  <script type="text/javascript" src="./src/index.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
</body>
</html>

JavaScript
Need to do some math with variable? Or want to have something monitor for a mouse click and then perform a task? JavaScript to the rescue! Looping through and pulling data from an object with thousands of pieces of information? JavaScript is at your service! It could loop through with only a few lines of code. JavaScript can even create HTML elements with the data that you just looped through with a createElement method.

This JavaScript code is using the forEach method to cycle through all of the menuData items and automatically creating HTML data that will display on the website when the buildMenu function is called. This could be hundreds of lines of code that would need to be manually entered in HTML all in a half dozen lines in JavaScript.

function buildMenu(menuData) {
    let menuList = document.querySelector("#menu-items");
    menuData.forEach(item => {
        let menuListItem = document.createElement("span");
        menuListItem.textContent = item.name;
        menuList.appendChild(menuListItem);
    });
}

How about if you had a comments section in your interface and wanted a user to be able to add those comments directly to the interface? JavaScript could be watching the “Post Comment” button for you to click on it after you have enter some text into the text entry form. It could store this data in a variable (or elsewhere) and tell the HTML to create a new paragraph with the text you just entered in the text entry form.

CSS
Opacity (transparency), grid alignment, assigning font families to particular regions of your page. Want your text boxes on the left half of the screen to have round edged borders? Let CSS easily tell the web browser how to display that.

While these properties would need to be manually entered to each item in HTML it can simplified into a few lines in CSS. One way to do that is to use a css selector to essentially filter all items of that type and assign the preferred visual characteristics. The CSS code here is selecting all p (paragraph) items and setting a handful of properties for them in a half dozen lines.

p {
      color: red;
      background-color: black;
      font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;
      font-weight: bold;
      border: #fff solid;
      border-radius: 5px;
    }

Want to be able to scale items appropriately and dynamically as you resize a window? You suspected correctly, CSS has you covered. I’ll spare you the details on how to do that, just know CSS makes it possible.

Summary
Hopefully you were able to get a tiny taste of what each of the 3 can do individually and see that they can compliment the others nicely to create great looking and powerful tools.

Hopefully the takeaway is:

HTML: primitive backbone or structure
JavaScript: engine that can calculate and automate complex tasks (and more!)
CSS: the super styler
All 3: much more powerful and efficient when combined together

Items.Title: 在 Python 中以编程方式创建子模块 | Programmatically create submodules in Python
Items.Description:
曼苏尔·穆菲德

发表于如果您曾经想将 Python包作为单个文件分发，但要保留点分命名空间的好处，这里有一个提示。

我们可以通过编程方式生成子模块，并使用importlib将它们添加到当前模块的命名空间中。

例如，这是一个带有名为y的子模块的单个文件模块（如果将其保存到名为x.py的文件中，则名为x ）：

 import importlib.machinery import importlib.util import sys spec = importlib.machinery.ModuleSpec('y', None) y = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec) sys.modules[__name__].y = y y.abc = 123

为了做import xy或from x import y就像x是一个包而不仅仅是一个模块，只需添加：

 sys.modules[__name__ + '.y'] = y

原文:

Mansour Moufid

Posted on Oct 11

If you've ever wanted to distribute a Python package as a single file, but keep the benefits of dotted namespaces, here is a tip.

We can generate submodules programmatically and add them to the current module's namespace using importlib.

For example, here is a single file module (named x if you save it to a file named x.py) with a submodule named y:

import importlib.machinery
import importlib.util
import sys

spec = importlib.machinery.ModuleSpec('y', None)
y = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
sys.modules[__name__].y = y

y.abc = 123

In order to do import x.y or from x import y as if x were a package and not simply a module, just add:

sys.modules[__name__ + '.y'] = y

Items.Description:
萨姆·萨尔加多

发表于工作量证明并不是一个新概念，它的开发是为了防止服务器滥用。 以比特币的形式，它被用作共识算法。 这种共识算法对于比特币保持中立并且没有单点故障是不可或缺的。 为了生产比特币，它依赖于矿工。 这些矿工生产加密货币，并因挖掘区块而获得区块奖励。 在工作量证明系统中，矿工必须解决一个复杂的问题才能挖掘区块。 与权益证明相反，矿工以交易费用的形式获得整体奖励。 有一些与此相关的问题，一个项目现在将有一个单点故障，并且该块是基于股份的，而不是基于优点的。 然而，比特币依赖于计算能力并激励矿工解决问题以获得奖励。 权益证明的另一个问题是这些项目是基于权益的，因此较贫穷的国家可能由于高昂的交易费用而无法参与。 工作量证明硬币依赖于计算能力而不是股份。 与工作量证明相关的问题是 51 攻击，在这种攻击中，如果一台计算机比其他节点拥有更多的计算能力，它就可以接管网络。 有了计算能力，随着越来越多的人进入网络，执行 51-49 攻击变得越困难。

Sam Salgado

Posted on Oct 11

Proof of work is not a novel concept and was developed to prevent server abuse. In the form of Bitcoin, it is used as a consensus algorithm. This consensus algorithm is integral for Bitcoin to remain neutral and have no single point of failure. In order for Bitcoin to be produced, it is dependent on miners. These miners produce the cryptocurrency and are rewarded with a block reward for mining the block. In a proof-of-work system, the miner must solve a complicated problem in order to mine the block. As opposed to proof-of-stake, in which miners receive a block reward in the form of transaction fees. There are some problems associated with that, a project will now have a single point of failure and the block is based on a stake, not on merit. Bitcoin, however, relies on computational power and incentivizes miners to solve the problem to get the reward. Another problem with proof-of-stake is that the projects are based on stake, so poorer nations might not be able to participate due to high transaction fees. Proof-of-work coins rely on computational power rather than a stake. The problem associated with proof-of-work is a 51 attack, in which if a computer has more computational power than the other nodes it can take over the network. With computational power, as more people get onto the network, the more difficult it becomes to execute a 51-49 attack.
